generated_oml:
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor providing torque for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, interfacing with gearbox and encoder systems."
]

instance SINUMERIKController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary control interface for the MABI MAX 100 robot executing motion commands and interfacing with sensor data streams via Siemens SINUMERIK controller."
]

instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular position feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned behind the gearbox providing output angle feedback for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time streaming of motor encoder data (input current and output angle) via MQTT protocol."
]

instance GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time streaming of gearbox encoder data (output angle) via MQTT protocol."
]

instance SysMLComponentConnectorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structural model of the robot's architecture using SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) capturing components (motors, gearboxes, encoders) and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance FrictionDynamicsSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable simulation model of the robot's friction dynamics within the first axis, developed in MathWorks MATLAB Simulink for motion control strategy refinement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance RobotSensorDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series sensor data traces (input current and output angle) collected from physical robot via MQTT for digital shadow purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, GearboxEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTEnabler
]

instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) used for structural modeling of the robot's architecture via component & connector diagrams (IBDs)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RobotArchitectureService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink employed for executable simulation models of the robot's friction dynamics to refine motion control strategies."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance MQTTEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT Protocol utilized for real-time streaming of sensor data traces (input current and output angle) from physical robot to digital shadow with low-latency communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, MonitoringService
]

instance SINUMERIKControlInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK Controller acting as primary control interface executing motion commands and interfacing with sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionControlService
]

instance RobotArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural modeling service providing component-connector architecture visualization of the MABI MAX 100 robot's first axis."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotArchitectureInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral simulation service for offline testing of control strategies and failure scenarios using friction dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionOptimizationInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service collecting and processing contextual data traces (sensor readings, actuator commands) into structured digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAggregationInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard-based visualization service for operational oversight of motor torque, gearbox temperature, and encoder feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalMonitoringInsight
]

instance MotionControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing motion commands through Siemens SINUMERIK controller interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionExecutionInsight
]

instance RobotArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural overview of the MABI MAX 100 robot's first axis components and their interconnections for system understanding."
]

instance MotionOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized motion profiles derived from friction dynamics simulation to improve robot motion accuracy."
]

instance DataAggregationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured digital representations of sensor data traces enabling contextual analysis for decision support."
]

instance OperationalMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational status visualization of the robot's first axis for manufacturing engineers and maintenance technicians."
]

instance MotionExecutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Execution status of motion commands through the SINUMERIK controller interface."
]

instance MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot for high-precision machining tasks."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, SINUMERIKController, MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder
]

instance FirstAxisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First vertical axis subsystem of MABI MAX 100 robot including motor, gearbox, and encoders for positional feedback."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearboxEncoder, MotorEncoder
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the serial robot MABI MAX 100 is modeled using **SysML** for structural and behavioral descriptions, including **internal block diagrams (IBDs)** for component-connector architectures and **state machines** (SysML v1.7) for behavioral modeling. The system integrates with **executable simulation models** (e.g., MathWorks MATLAB Simulink) for dynamic behavior representation. No explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or resource management mechanisms are detailed, but the system relies on **heterogeneous tool integration** (SysML + Simulink) to unify modeling and simulation. The document does not mention architecture patterns like ACME, AADL, or MontiArc for this specific use case."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs **MathWorks Simulink** for modeling **friction within the first axis** and **rigidity in robot joints**, using executable simulation models to replicate physical behavior. **Validation** is performed by comparing **simulated data against measured data** to quantify deviations and ensure model accuracy. No explicit mention of uncertainty quantification, error detection mechanisms (e.g., threshold-based anomaly detection), or quality assurance processes is provided in the document."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the serial robot MABI MAX 100 integrates with external systems via **MQTT** for streaming time-series data from the physical robot axis to the digital shadow. The system leverages **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** as a standardized interface for data exchange, enabling interoperability with enterprise systems or other Digital Twins. No explicit dependencies on protocols like OPC UA or REST are mentioned, but the use of MQTT and AAS implies adherence to **Industry 4.0 interoperability standards**. The document does not specify integration patterns (e.g., publish-subscribe, API gateways) beyond the MQTT-AAS pipeline."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** standards, including **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** for system architecture and behavior modeling. For **architecture description**, it leverages **ADLs (Architecture Description Languages)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, and **MontiArc**, which are standardized for component-based system design. **SysML v1.7** supports state machines for behavioral modeling, aligning with **OMG (Object Management Group) standards**. Interoperability is facilitated through integration with **MathWorks Simulink** and other simulation tools, though no specific interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) are mentioned. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) implies compliance with **manufacturing-specific data aggregation standards**, but no explicit certification or compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 23247, IEC 62443) are referenced. The document does not specify **industry standards** (e.g., NIST, IEEE) or **compliance requirements** beyond MBSE and ADL methodologies."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical mechanisms** such as the **safety cell** (as referenced in the MABI robotic arm documentation [44]), which restricts the working range of the robotic system to prevent collisions and ensure operator safety. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [47], [50]) provides a standardized structure for integrating safety-related metadata, operational constraints, and compliance requirements for industrial assets. Additionally, **data streaming protocols** like **MQTT** ([52]) are employed for real-time communication, though explicit cybersecurity measures (e.g., encryption, authentication protocols) are not detailed in the provided documents. The **RAMI4.0 reference architecture** ([48], [49]) aligns with Industry 4.0 safety standards but does not specify granular security controls. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied through the use of structured simulation models (e.g., MathWorks MATLAB Simulink for executable models [43]) to validate safety-critical behaviors, but no explicit threat mitigation strategies or risk assessment methodologies are documented."
]
